releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
Reliability release for large-vault sync.

- Fixed HTTP 429 rate-limit floods during full library sync on large vaults (2,500+ archives): linked-archive lookups now use one bulk index request instead of one request per archive, and all sync retries honor the server's `Retry-After`.
- Added a shared client-side request throttle so library sync, linked-archive sync, and mobile queue sync no longer compete for the same server rate-limit budget.
- Mobile sync queue items are no longer permanently stranded when a rate limit hits mid-sync — they stay pending and retry automatically; per-item failure notices are collapsed into a single summary.
- Fixed recurring "File already exists." errors during author profile sync on case-insensitive file systems and when notes are created concurrently.

releases.author: hyungyunlim
releases.description:
Adds the **Reader AI Chat bridge** so the Social Archiver Chrome extension's Reader Mode can chat with the Claude Code CLI running on your Mac. The existing media features (`dash_mux`, `hls_remux`) are unchanged and fully backward compatible.

## New

- **AI chat endpoints** — new `ai_chat` capability with `GET /ai/providers` (detects your local Claude Code CLI) and `POST /ai/chat` (SSE stream). Same pairing-token auth as the media endpoints. The extension's reader drawer talks to these to answer questions about the article you're reading, using your own Claude Code login.
- **Model selection** — the drawer's model picker (Default / Sonnet / Opus / Haiku) is passed through to the CLI.
- **Session continuity** — follow-up turns resume the same CLI session, so the assistant remembers earlier messages in the conversation.

## Reliability

- CLI detection is **pre-warmed at startup** and served stale-while-revalidate, so the first chat after a helper restart no longer times out on the one-time cold spawn cost (previously this could make the drawer falsely show "Install the local helper").

## Security

The article text is treated as untrusted web content. The spawned CLI runs with **all tools disabled** (`--disallowedTools`), from an empty scratch working directory (no project settings loaded), under a wall-clock timeout, with a cap of 2 concurrent chats.

## Requirements

- macOS. AI chat additionally requires the [Claude Code CLI](https://claude.com/claude-code) installed and signed in. Chats run on your own Claude Code login and count toward your plan.

## Install

Download and open `social-archiver-local-media-helper-0.2.0-macos-arm64.pkg` below — it is signed with a Developer ID and notarized by Apple. After installing, pair it from the extension's Options page.
releases.name: Local Media Helper v0.2.0

## Bulk import to your vault — Reddit, X & Instagram

Import whole collections straight into your vault from the [Social Archiver Chrome extension](https://chromewebstore.google.com/detail/social-archiver/gifddgdekeffpnadpdcgjaehmlblomaf) — no account, no quota.

- **Bulk local import** — Reddit saved posts, X bookmarks, and Instagram saved/your posts land as vault notes in one run, media included. Zero server calls; never uses your monthly archive quota.
- **Resilient** — close Obsidian mid-import and it resumes on next launch. Hit a rate limit and the posts collected so far are saved right away, with the rest continuing on resume.
**Fixes:** bulk-delete hover contrast · per-device sync client identity · LinkedIn local clipping.

Requires Chrome extension 1.6.3+. See the [browser extension guide](https://docs.social-archive.org/en/guide/browser-extension) for setup.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## Browser clips & local-only mode

Clip posts straight into your vault from the [Social Archiver Chrome extension](https://chromewebstore.google.com/detail/social-archiver/gifddgdekeffpnadpdcgjaehmlblomaf) — no account needed.

- **Local-only by design** — clips never touch the server and never use your monthly archive quota.
- **Import anytime** — once signed in, move local clips into your account (media included) from Settings → Local archives, or right-click a clip note → "Upload archive to account". Paid plans can auto-upload new clips.
- **Works fully logged out** — timeline, tags, and settings no longer require an account.
- **Chrome extension 1.6.1+** unlocks the full clip platform set: X, Threads, Instagram, Facebook, Reddit, TikTok, Bluesky, Mastodon, LinkedIn, Substack notes, Naver Blog/Cafe, Kakao Brunch, and ordinary web pages.

See the [browser extension guide](https://docs.social-archive.org/en/guide/browser-extension) for setup.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## Comment pins & deletions now sync to your notes

- Pinned comments stand out with a clear **📌 Pinned** accent badge in the timeline, and a collapsed thread that contains a pin shows a **📌 Pinned reply** hint so the pin is discoverable before you expand it.
- Pinned comments sort above the rest of the thread and stay visible even under the default "show last 2" collapse — a pinned-but-older comment is never hidden.
- Pin state round-trips through the note's markdown, so the pin survives reopening, reloading, and re-sync.
- Comments removed on the platform (or from another device) are now deleted from the archived note when comment state syncs.
- Comment state reconciles on delta and focus catch-up, so pins and deletions that arrived while Obsidian was in the background (missed real-time events) are recovered the next time the note or app regains focus.
- Media hardening: archived notes no longer leak internal media-path placeholders; unavailable media renders a clean block, and a sentinel region lets media be repaired automatically on a re-run.

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/hyungyunlim/obsidian-social-archiver-releases/compare/3.9.1...3.10.0

## Substack Notes

Full Substack Notes support, plus fixes for how shared/quoted posts are archived.

- **Notes archived directly** — full text with author name, avatar, and bio; likes/restacks/replies counts.
- **Threaded comments** — a note's reply thread is captured and rendered with nesting, and comment authors now link to their profile.
- **Images** download into your vault and show as a **carousel** (matching mobile), instead of a stacked article layout.
- **Videos** are preserved as MP4 so they play, instead of showing an unplayable streaming link.
- **Quoted highlights** — when a note restacks a post with a highlighted passage, the quote renders as a blockquote linking to the source post.
- **Shared posts** opened in the Notes UI (`/note/p-…`) and app post links (`/@handle/p-…`) are now archived as full articles instead of failing.
- Substack **notes vs articles/blogs** are distinguished so each renders appropriately.

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/hyungyunlim/obsidian-social-archiver-releases/compare/3.8.3...3.8.4

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## Beta is wrapping up

Thanks for being part of the Social Archiver beta. The free beta period is **wrapping up in the next few days**, and the plan structure will look like this:

- Existing beta users will **automatically transition to the Free plan** — no action needed.
- The **Free plan includes 10 archives per month**. Earn more by completing in-app missions on the mobile app's Rewards screen.
- Subscriptions and the lifetime offer are available **only through the mobile app**, since Obsidian community plugin policy doesn't allow in-plugin payments. To upgrade or restore an existing purchase, sign in on the mobile app with the same email — it will recognize your account.

Get the mobile app: https://social-archive.org/get-mobile (auto-routes to App Store / Play Store)

## In-App Notices (new)

The plugin can now receive **server-published announcements** in the timeline (above the existing sync/crawl banners) without waiting for a plugin update.

- Title-only line at first; tap to expand the full message in a detail sheet.
- Dismissals persist across plugin restarts (and across desktops if your vault is synced via Obsidian Sync / iCloud / Git).
- Operators can roll out announcements gradually and target specific surfaces — used for things like new feature heads-up, billing changes, and maintenance windows.

## Improvements

- **Comment mention rendering** — \`@mentions\` inside archived comments now render as proper links across all timeline and Markdown views.
- **Tier-aware rate limiting** groundwork — the plugin now understands \`Retry-After\` and \`X-RateLimit-*\` headers so transient throttling shows a clean message instead of a generic error.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:

### Publisher icons for web archives
- Web archives from recognized publications now show the publication's own icon — NYT, The Guardian, TechCrunch, Substack, Medium, The Atlantic, Bloomberg, and more — across the timeline, post cards, and headers, instead of the generic web icon. 34 publishers in the initial set, with URL-based lookup for archives saved before this version.

### Archive quota in settings
- Plugin settings now show your monthly archive quota and current usage.

## Minor changes and fixes
- Fixed Naver news articles (`n.news.naver.com`, `m.news.naver.com`) saving as raw HTML page dumps. They now extract clean article content like any other web article.
- Fixed the plugin inbox not showing the empty state after a bulk archive completes.

FYI. Instagram import added chrome version 1.3.0 is still under review process.. taking longer than usual

releases.author: github-actions[bot]

## Major changes

### Instagram Saved Posts import 
- You first need to archive your instagram saved posts through [Chrome extension ](https://chromewebstore.google.com/detail/social-archiver/gifddgdekeffpnadpdcgjaehmlblomaf?authuser=0&hl=ko)1.3.0 which are going through review process for now.  There are two options for importing, either download zip file and import through Obsidian, or directly upload through API. The Obsidian option has more grangular control over importing process although direct upload flow is simple and easier. Recommended to import a collection with smaller size.
- Added the first full Obsidian import flow for Instagram Saved Posts exports.
- Added import preflight with destination selection, job-wide tags, progress, completion, and failure views.
- Added vault attachment materialization so imported media, thumbnails, and author avatars are written into the vault and referenced with local paths.

### Review before importing
- Added a review gallery so you can choose which saved posts to import instead of importing everything blindly.
- Added collection filtering, search across caption/author/shortcode/collection, per-post selection, per-author bulk selection, and bulk actions.
- Added a media preview cache and card layout that mirrors the final timeline rendering more closely.

## Minor changes and fixes

- Fixed synced Obsidian highlights where selections with edge whitespace rendered as literal `==highlight==` text.
- Fixed reader-mode heading sizing so h5/h6 no longer render smaller than body text.
- Fixed mobile/library/realtime sync media handling so `Download media: Images only` no longer downloads video files into the vault.
- Added structured logging around share/archive orphan-candidate paths to make production drift easier to diagnose.
- Synced platform definitions and icons with the latest shared platform metadata.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## Reddit: Full Comment Tree

Archives now save **the entire comment discussion** — all nesting depths and collapsed branches — instead of just the first few replies.

- Nested replies render with progressive indentation (`↳ ↳ ↳`)
- Collapsed branches are auto-expanded via Reddit's `morechildren` API
- Comment permalinks resolve targets inside deep sub-trees
- Deleted comments with live replies are preserved as structural anchors
- Capped at 500 comments

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
### Unified highlight sync across every client
Highlights you make in the plugin, mobile app, desktop app, or on the web now all share the same coordinate system — so a highlight you create on your phone shows up in the exact same spot on your desktop and in your Obsidian vault. Older highlights are silently upgraded the first time you open them. (IOS needs approval for next release 1.5.3)

### Large-media guard for your vault
When you archive a post with an unusually heavy image or video, the plugin now asks before saving it locally — you can keep it in the vault or detach it and re-download on demand. Useful for subscription-heavy vaults that were quietly ballooning past several gigabytes.

### Improved archiving for SPA pages
Pages that used to return blank content (Notion pages, some modern sites) now fall back to a full browser render so the text and screenshots come through correctly.

### Faster share links
Creating a share link no longer re-uploads images from your vault — it reuses the media already preserved on the server. Share creation is noticeably faster, especially on mobile or slow connections, and the success message now tells you exactly what was reused vs. newly uploaded.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]

## What's New in 3.2.4

releases.description:
## 3.2.1 — Sync Stability & Data Protection

This release improves the reliability of multi-device sync and adds safeguards to protect your archived data.

### Improvements

- **Improved sync safety** — Enhanced how archive deletions are synchronized across devices to prevent unintended data loss
- **Better conflict handling** — Vault file management (moving, reorganizing notes) no longer risks affecting your server-side archives
- **Confirmation prompt** — A confirmation dialog now appears before syncing local file deletions to the server
- **Smarter sync defaults** — Outbound delete sync is now opt-in (Settings → Sync) for safer out-of-the-box experience
- **Clear Queue button** — You can now clear pending server deletes directly from Settings → Sync

### Bug Fixes

- Fixed an issue where reconnecting to the sync server could process a large backlog of pending operations at once
- Fixed an edge case where deleting a vault file could affect multiple server archives with the same URL
- Fixed YAML parse error when author bio contains multi-line text
- Improved echo suppression for archive deletion events across connected devices
- Disabling outbound delete sync now automatically clears the pending queue

### Notes

If you previously had outbound delete sync enabled, your existing setting is preserved. New installations default to disabled. You can manage this in **Settings → Sync → Delete Sync**.

releases.description:
## What's New in 3.1.0

### Author Notes ([#16](https://github.com/hyungyunlim/obsidian-social-archiver-releases/issues/16))
- **Vault-native author notes** — per-author markdown files with YAML frontmatter for profile metadata and a free-form body for your personal notes. Enable in Settings → Author.
- **Auto-create on archive** — author notes are created or updated automatically when you archive a post. Only frontmatter is touched; your notes stay intact.
- **Bulk generation** — "Scan & Generate" button in Settings → Author creates notes for all existing authors at once. Safe to run multiple times.
- **Identity-based dedup** — notes are keyed by canonical author URL. Handle/display name changes reuse the existing note. Legacy name-based keys auto-promote when a stable URL becomes available.
- **Note body preview** — your notes appear inline in Author Catalog (expanded row), Author Detail header, and as a hover tooltip on author names in the timeline.

### Author Detail View
- **Opens in main tab** — clicking an author name now opens a full Author Detail tab instead of inline sidebar. Each author gets its own tab.
- **Persists across reloads** — author tabs survive plugin reload with deferred state restoration.

### Author Card View
- **New card view** — masonry grid layout for the Authors list. Toggle between list and card view from the Authors header.
- Compact cards with centered avatar, name, handle, platform badge, subscription status dot, stats, and bio preview.
- Responsive columns (1–4) based on pane width.

### Hashtag Tag Control ([#15](https://github.com/hyungyunlim/obsidian-social-archiver-releases/issues/15))
- **New toggle**: Settings → Archive → "Include hashtags as Obsidian tags". When off, hashtags render as plain-text links instead of native `#tags`, keeping your tag namespace clean.

### Tag Fixes ([#17](https://github.com/hyungyunlim/obsidian-social-archiver-releases/issues/17))
- Fixed duplicate tag creation (`#tag` vs `tag` variants). Tag input now strips `#` prefix automatically.
- **Tag color sync** — tags created on mobile now sync their color definitions to the desktop plugin. Previously, tags from mobile appeared without colors because there was no inbound tag sync.
- Outbound tag sync now includes color data so colors stay consistent across devices.

### Other
- "Subscriptions" renamed to "Authors"; Settings reorganized (Author section above Archive).
- Cross-device sync: persistent dirty tracking, WebSocket action_updated handler for like/bookmark/annotation/shareUrl, queue-driven dirty lifecycle, infinite reconnect with backoff + jitter, dynamic delta sync cooldown, share URL sync.
- iOS-style mobile search bar; fixed stuck hover/active states on toolbar buttons; fixed gallery toggle spinner stuck; fixed sort button active state; shortened vertical connector lines; media placeholder skip during preservation.

## What's New in 2.9.2

### Mobile-to-Desktop Sync

Your highlights, notes, tags, and deletions from the mobile app now sync to your Obsidian vault in real-time.

- **Notes sync** — Notes you add on mobile automatically appear in the `comment` property of the matching vault note. Multiple notes are joined together.
- **Tags sync** — Tags added on mobile are merged into the `tags` property. Your existing local tags are preserved.
- **Delete sync** — When you delete an archive on mobile, the corresponding vault file is moved to your system trash (recoverable).

All sync features are behind a new **Mobile Annotation Sync** toggle in Settings → Sync. Turn it on to get started.

### Improved WebSocket Connection

The plugin now connects to the authenticated (private) WebSocket channel, which means all real-time events — including new archives, action updates, and deletions — are delivered instantly instead of relying on periodic polling. 

### Timeline Card Refresh

When a note's metadata changes (comment, tags, like status), the timeline card now updates immediately without needing to scroll away and back.

---

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/hyungyunlim/obsidian-social-archiver-releases/compare/2.9.1...2.9.2

releases.author: hyungyunlim
releases.description:
## Highlights

### Video Transcription with Local Whisper
- **Video transcript player**: Archive a YouTube or TikTok video, and the transcript appears as a synced, scrollable panel alongside the embedded video with playback controls.
- **Transcription suggestion banner**: When a local video is detected in your note, a banner prompts you to transcribe it using your local Whisper installation (faster-whisper / whisper.cpp / openai-whisper) with model selection and time estimates.
- **Native closed captions (CC)**: Local videos now support TextTrack-based captions with a CC toggle button, synced to the Whisper transcript.
- **Multilingual transcript translation**: Translate transcripts into other languages and switch between them via pill-style language tabs.

### Batch Download & Transcription
- **Batch processing**: Scan your archive folder and batch-download + transcribe all eligible videos with a single command. Supports pause, resume, and cancel with persistent progress tracking across plugin reloads.
- **Improved batch notice UI**: Progress bar with status-aware colors, icon+label buttons with proper spacing, and auto-dismiss on completion.

### Archive Progress Banner (replaces Preliminary Document)
- **New loading UX**: Instead of creating a placeholder document during archiving, a non-intrusive progress banner now appears at the top of the timeline showing real-time status for each job (queued, archiving, retrying, completed, failed).
- **Dismiss & retry**: Every banner state has a dismiss (X) button; failed jobs show a retry button. Dismissed jobs are properly cleaned up and won't reappear on reload.

## Also Included (from 2.5.7)
- Fixed Facebook Reels being saved as thumbnails instead of actual video by prioritizing `r2Url → url → cdnUrl`.
- Improved `.mp4` extension handling for ambiguous video URLs.
- Improved mobile sync resilience: transient `Archive not found` responses now trigger retry instead of failure.
- Unified frontmatter property ordering and improved archive tag strategy with `tagRoot` and `tagOrganization` options.
- Added customizable frontmatter property names for multi-language support.
- Added video download failure UX: red status message, checkbox property, and toast notification.
- Added pre-capture warning when `IMAGE+VIDEO` mode is selected.

Generate transcripts for podcast episodes using local Whisper AI. All processing happens on your device.

| [faster-whisper](https://github.com/SYSTRAN/faster-whisper) | ❌ No | [CLI wrapper required](https://docs.social-archive.org/en/guide/transcription#option-3-faster-whisper) |

### Short Link Expansion
Pinterest pin.it short links are now automatically expanded to full URLs. Since pin.it links don't indicate whether they point to a single pin or a board, the plugin now resolves these links first to determine the correct content type and archiving flow.

### Board Detection
Improved handling for Pinterest board URLs. The archive modal now properly detects and handles Pinterest boards, showing appropriate guidance when a board URL is entered instead of a single pin.

### Embed Rendering
Fixed Pinterest embed rendering in archived posts. Pinterest content now displays with proper formatting and layout in your notes.

## HEIC/HEIF Upgrade

### HDR Image Support
Upgraded to libheif-js 1.19.8 for improved HEIC/HEIF image handling, including support for HDR images from newer iPhones.

### Better Fallback
Added native browser decoder as a fallback option, improving reliability when converting HEIC images to JPEG format.

Archive Tumblr posts with full support for hashtag timeline search. Supports regular posts, reblogs, and rich media content with proper tag preservation.

### HEIC/HEIF Image Support
Full compatibility with iOS and macOS native image formats (HEIC/HEIF). Images are automatically converted to JPEG for universal compatibility across all platforms and devices.

### Enhanced Media Handling
- Automatic format detection and conversion for HEIC images
- Improved image optimization pipeline with format fallback handling
- Better MIME type detection across all image formats
- Enhanced preview support in MediaAttacher and PostComposer

releases.description:
## Social Archiver v1.0.3 - Initial Public Beta

Archive social media posts from 8 major platforms directly into your Obsidian vault.

### Features

**Multi-Platform Support**
- Facebook (Posts, Photos, Videos)
- LinkedIn (Posts, Articles)
- Instagram (Posts, Reels, Stories)
- TikTok (Videos with transcript extraction)
- X.com / Twitter (Tweets, Threads, Moments, Spaces)
- Threads (Posts)
- YouTube (Videos with transcripts - raw and formatted)
- Reddit (Posts and Comments)

**Advanced Archiving**
- Instant document creation with background processing
- Non-blocking async job processing with retry logic
- Real-time WebSocket notifications
- Three media download modes: text-only, images-only, or full media preservation
- Automatic link preview extraction
- Embedded archives for quote posts

**Timeline View**
- Custom Obsidian view (sidebar or full-screen)
- Advanced filtering by platform, date range, and status
- Full-text search across post content
- Inline post composer

**Authentication & Security**
- Magic link passwordless authentication
- JWT token-based API access
- Multi-device support
- Protocol handler for mobile share extension

**Configuration**
- Customizable archive and media folder paths
- File naming with tokens: {date}, {platform}, {author}, {slug}
- Usage statistics and timing metrics

### Recent Optimizations

- MetadataCache integration for 70% faster file operations
- Enhanced performance metrics
- Improved error handling and retry logic
- Fixed quote post extraction
- Better metadata handling

### Beta Access

Currently in beta with unlimited free archiving for early adopters. Help us improve by reporting bugs and suggesting features.

### Installation

1. Download main.js, manifest.json, and styles.css
2. Create folder: <vault>/.obsidian/plugins/social-archiver/
3. Extract files to that folder
4. Reload Obsidian and enable the plugin

### Documentation

- [Full README](https://github.com/hyungyunlim/obsidian-social-archiver-releases#readme)
- [Release Guide](https://github.com/hyungyunlim/obsidian-social-archiver-releases/blob/main/RELEASE_GUIDE.md)

### Important Note

Only archive content you have permission to save. Respect copyright and privacy laws. This tool is for personal archiving only.

---

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/hyungyunlim/obsidian-social-archiver-releases/commits/1.0.3
